skill_text:
A powerful web crawler that initiates a structured web crawl starting from a specified base URL. The crawler expands from that point like a tree, following internal links across pages. You can control how deep and wide it goes, and guide it to focus on specific sections of the site. Provide the required field `url`.
When to use:
- Use `tavily-crawl` when the user's request directly matches this tool's purpose.
- Provide the required field `url`.
- Optional controls include `max_depth`, `max_breadth`, `limit`, `instructions`, and other optional fields.
- Use examples to map paraphrases into schema-faithful arguments.
When not to use:
- Do not call this tool when required inputs are missing or ambiguous.
- Do not invent unsupported parameters or unsupported enum values.
- Respect the allowed values for `extract_depth`: 'basic', 'advanced'.
Argument template:
{"allow_external": false, "categories": [], "extract_depth": "basic", "format": "markdown", "instructions": "sample_instructions_4", "limit": 50, "max_breadth": 20, "max_depth": 1, "select_domains": [], "select_paths": [], "url": "https://example.com/resource"}
Examples:
Minimal valid request that satisfies the required fields for tavily-crawl
{"url": "https://example.com/resource"}
Richer invocation that uses optional controls for tavily-crawl
{"allow_external": false, "categories": [], "extract_depth": "basic", "format": "markdown", "instructions": "sample_instructions_2", "limit": 50, "max_breadth": 20, "max_depth": 1, "select_domains": [], "select_paths": [], "url": "https://example.com/resource"}
Required-argument dev behavior example.
{"url": "https://example.com/resource"}
Optional, enum, nested, or array argument example.
{"allow_external": false, "categories": [], "extract_depth": "basic", "format": "markdown", "instructions": "sample_instructions_4", "limit": 50, "max_breadth": 20, "max_depth": 1, "select_domains": [], "select_paths": [], "url": "https://example.com/resource"}
